TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi beforefieldinit ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkingSecurity extends System.Object implements class System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel`1<class Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkingSecurity>, class System.ClientModel.Primitives.IPersistableModel`1<class Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkingSecurity>
Public Class ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkingSecurity Implements IJsonModel(Of ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkingSecurity), IPersistableModel(Of ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkingSecurity)

Docs.summary: Security profile to enable security features on cilium based cluster.

MemberSignature.Value: public ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkingSecurity ();
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed
Public Sub New ()
public Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkPolicy? AdvancedNetworkPolicies { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Nullable`1<valuetype Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkPolicy> AdvancedNetworkPolicies
Public Property AdvancedNetworkPolicies As Nullable(Of ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkPolicy)
member this.AdvancedNetworkPolicies : Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ManagedClusterAdvancedNetworkPolicy> with get, set

Docs.summary: Enable advanced network policies. This allows users to configure Layer 7 network policies (FQDN, HTTP, Kafka). Policies themselves must be configured via the Cilium Network Policy resources, see https://docs.cilium.io/en/latest/security/policy/index.html. This can be enabled only on cilium-based clusters. If not specified, the default value is FQDN if security.enabled is set to true.

MemberSignature.Value: public bool? IsEnabled { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Nullable`1<bool> IsEnabled
Public Property IsEnabled As Nullable(Of Boolean)
member this.IsEnabled : Nullable<bool> with get, set

Docs.summary: This feature allows user to configure network policy based on DNS (FQDN) names. It can be enabled only on cilium based clusters. If not specified, the default is false.

MemberSignature.Value: public Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.TransitEncryptionType? TransitEncryptionType { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Nullable`1<valuetype Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.TransitEncryptionType> TransitEncryptionType
Public Property TransitEncryptionType As Nullable(Of TransitEncryptionType)
member this.TransitEncryptionType : Nullable<Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.TransitEncryptionType> with get, set

Docs.summary: Configures pod-to-pod encryption. This can be enabled only on Cilium-based clusters. If not specified, the default value is None.
